titlestmt.titleproper: Finding aid for the Henry Goodman collection of theater playbills LSC.2286

Finding aid for accession LSC-2286-1995-004 prepared by Sadie Baldwin with assistance from Emily Wittenberg in the Center for Primary Research Training (CFPRT), June 2026.

did.unittitle: Henry Goodman collection of theater playbills
did.unitdate: circa 1762 – circa early-1900s, 1951; bulk dates, circa 1830s-1878
did.abstract: Henry Goodman, Ph.D., faculty emeritus at UCLA, had a distinguished career as a professor of theater. The collection, assembled by Goodman, consists of 18th, 19th, and 20th century theater playbills and ephemera, with the bulk dating from the 1830s through the 1870s. The collection includes British theater playbills, a substantial group of American theater playbills, a small number of foreign language playbills, five scrapbooks, and a small amount of illustrations and photographs.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Provenance/Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Professor Henry Goodman, 1990 and 1995.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Henry Goodman collection of theater playbills (Collection 2286). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Jenifer Becker with assistance from Julie Graham, March 2016. Accession LSC-2286-1995-004 processed by Sadie Baldwin with assistance from Emily Wittenberg in the Center for Primary Research Training (CFPRT), June 2026.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's Library Collections and Archives.

bioghist.head: Biography/History
bioghist.p: Henry Goodman was born November 21, 1919 in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Goodman received his BA (1941), MA (1947), and Ph.D. (1952) from the University of Minnesota. From 1953-1956, he taught at Rockford College in Illinois and at Reed College in Oregon. In 1957, Goodman joined the faculty at University of California, Los Angeles, where he taught and directed until his retirement in 1990. Among his many accomplishments, he introduced and directed the world premiere of Bertolt Brecht's The Caucasian Chalk Circle at Carleton College. Henry Goodman died April 15, 2012 in Los Angeles, California.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of 18th, 19th, and 20th theater playbills and ephemera collected by UCLA professor of theater Henry Goodman. The bulk of the collection consists of 19th-century British and American theater playbills. British theaters represented include Astley's Royal Amphitheatre, Lyceum Theatre, Nottingham Empire, Royal Princess's Theater, Surrey Theatre, Theatre Royal, Covent-Garden, Theatre Royal, Drury Lane, and others. The collection also includes a substantial group of American theater playbills, chiefly from the 1860s and 1870s. Venues represented include Academy of Music, Booth's Theatre, Broadway Theatre, Daly's Fifth Avenue Theatre, French Theatre, Niblo's Garden, Olympic Theatre, Park Theatre, Pike's Opera House, Providence Opera House, Wallack's Theatre, Winter Garden, and other theaters. Additionally included are one Russian language playbill, one French language playbill, and four items representing Yiddish theater printed in English and what appears to be Hebrew. Also included are five scrapbooks representing 19th and early 20th century theater, as well as a small amount of illustrations and photographs.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
The collection is organized in the following series:
1. Playbills
2. Scrapbooks, Illustrations, and Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of 18th, 19th, and 20th century British and American theater playbills. The bulk of the series consists of 19th century playbills and includes both color and black and white specimens. British playbills represent performances from Astley's Royal Amphitheatre, Lyceum Theatre, Nottingham Empire, Royal Princess's Theater, Surrey Theatre, Theatre Royal, Covent-Garden, Theatre Royal, Drury Lane, and other theaters. The series also includes a substantial group of American theater playbills, chiefly dating from the 1860s and 1870s. These materials represent performances at Academy of Music, Booth's Theatre, Broadway Theatre, Daly's Fifth Avenue Theatre, French Theatre, Niblo's Garden, Olympic Theatre, Park Theatre, Pike's Opera House, Providence Opera House, Wallack's Theatre, Winter Garden, and other venues. A large portion of the American material documents productions at Wallack's Theatre. Additionally included is one Russian language playbill, one French language playbill, and four items representing Yiddish theater that are printed in English and what appears to be Hebrew. Among the many performers represented are Edwin Booth, Charlotte Cushman, Charles Fechter, Joseph Jefferson, Clara Morris, Adelaide Neilson, Frederick B. Warde, Lester Wallack, John Brougham, John Gilbert, J. H. Stoddart, Charles Fisher, Charles Rockwell, Madame Vestris, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by theater or venue name. Playbills without an identified theater or venue are arranged alphabetically by production title. Multiple playbills from the same theater are generally arranged alphabetically by production title, with dated and undated materials filed together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of five scrapbooks and loose illustrations and photographs. The scrapbooks represent 19th and early 20th century theatre. Two scrapbooks contain illustrations/caricatures and photographs; one scrapbook is primarily autographs; one scrapbook is cut outs from American theater playbills; and one scrapbook consists of cut out characters for toy theatre representing Robinson Crusoe, Black-eyed Susan, and Hamlet. The loose illustrations and photographs represent performers, theatres, and costumes. Additionally, included is one letter from Macready to Count D'Orsay.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged alphabetically by title or name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Full Dress of a Gentleman; Half Dress; and Promenade Dress.
